headline: Australian Plastics Recycling Company Targets Critical Minerals
summary: An Australian startup that developed plastic-eating enzymes is looking to raise more than $70 million as it focuses its recycling technology on critical minerals.  Since 2021, Samsara Eco has used artificial intelligence to design new enzymes that can break down commonly used types of plastics, allowing them to be reused in new products.  The company, which is backed by Singapore state-investment company Temasek and has a supply deal with apparel maker  Lululemon  plans to tailor microscopic organisms to retrieve materials from electronic waste otherwise destined for landfills.

headline: Fabletics launches denim as the athleisure trade loses steam
summary: Fabletics is launching a new denim assortment priced between $79.95 and $174.95 as the athleisure boom starts to slow down.

headline: Why athleisure brand Fabletics is betting on denim
summary: Athleisure giant Fabletics is expanding into denim. It signals a bigger shift in consumer tastes as shoppers move away from yoga pants and back toward jeans. With 30% of its over 3 million subscribers expressing interest in denim, Fabletics is betting on premium jeans at accessible prices for its members and potential new customers.
